title: HarnessTemplate
description: Declarative bundle of guides, sensors, and their pairings for a specific substrate topology. Defined by ADR-004 and PRD-harness-engineering M2.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this schema for editor tooling

version.description: Semantic version of this template instance

topology.description: Named topology this template governs (e.g. agentbox-agent-task, governance-decision)

maturity.description: Maturity level: planned (design only), scaffolded (schema exists), standalone (works in isolation), integrated (cross-substrate wired), verified (smoke-tested end-to-end)

structure.description: Declares what exists in this topology: substrates, event kinds, and data flow graph

substrates.description: Repository substrates involved in this topology

event_kinds.description: Nostr event kinds consumed or produced (if applicable)

data_flows.description: Directed data movement edges between substrates or components

guides.description: Feedforward controls that shape behaviour before acting

sensors.description: Feedback controls that observe and correct after acting

pairings.description: Bindings between guides and sensors declaring validation discipline

escalation_rules.description: Actions triggered when sensor readings cross thresholds

protocol.description: Wire protocol or transport (e.g. nostr-relay, HTTP-LDP, MCP, SPARQL)

description.description: Human-readable description of what flows along this edge

type.description: Classification: instruction (prose/config), schema (JSON Schema/OWL), constraint (invariant/threshold), hook (executable pre-check), ontology_axiom (OWL 2 EL axiom)

source.description: File path, MCP tool name, or URL where the guide is defined

source_status.description: Whether the source resolves to a real artifact in the named substrate today. present (default when omitted): the file/tool exists. planned: the guide is declared but its source does not yet exist — the pairing is aspirational, not backed by code.

description.description: What this guide constrains or instructs

type.description: Computational: deterministic, fast, CPU-bound. Inferential: probabilistic, slower, LLM/GPU-bound.

source.description: Test file, MCP tool, CI script, or hook that implements this sensor

source_status.description: Whether the source resolves to a real artifact in the named substrate today. present (default when omitted): the test/tool/monitor exists. planned: the sensor is declared but its implementation does not yet exist — the pairing is aspirational, not backed by code.

frequency.description: How often the sensor runs: per_change (on save/edit), per_commit (CI), per_release (release gate), continuous (runtime monitor)

description.description: What this sensor measures or validates

guide_id.description: Reference to a Guide.id in this template

sensor_id.description: Reference to a Sensor.id in this template

validation_mode.description: blocking: sensor failure prevents progression. advisory: sensor reports but does not block. learning: sensor feeds the learning service for future improvement.

sensor_id.description: Reference to a Sensor.id in this template

threshold.description: Value or condition that triggers escalation (type depends on sensor)

action.description: What happens when the threshold is crossed
